name: Alps to Ocean
description: A scenic South Island journey with mostly approachable terrain.
difficultyExplanation: This route uses well-maintained trail and road links with regular access to towns, campsites, and food. Gradients are moderate for most riders and route finding is straightforward.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: Day one from Aoraki to Lake Pukaki can be long and brutal into a southerly wind across the open lake plain.
routeSummary: Alps to Ocean is a easy bikepacking route covering about 299 km, commonly ridden over 4 days. A scenic South Island journey with mostly approachable terrain. Riders can expect 60% Gravel / 35% Road / 5% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: Day one from Aoraki to Lake Pukaki can be long and brutal into a southerly wind across the open lake plain. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 4-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
